content: Haystack pipelines connect reusable retrieval and generation components.
Hydra composes experiment configuration from defaults, config groups, and command line overrides.
Retrieval experiments usually separate indexing documents from querying and metric evaluation.

Long retrieval documents often need a preparation pipeline before dense retrieval works well. A realistic indexing workflow starts with raw source records, cleans irregular whitespace and control characters, splits long passages into overlapping chunks, and adds the E5 passage prefix to every chunk before embedding. Chunking helps keep each vector focused on one coherent topic while overlap keeps useful context near chunk boundaries. After chunkization, the document embedder produces one embedding per chunk and the indexer persists the chunk id, text, metadata, and embedding. During inference, the query is cleaned, prefixed with the E5 query prefix, embedded with the same model family, and compared against the saved chunk embeddings. This separation lets researchers rerun retrieval quickly, inspect saved artifacts, and later swap in richer stores such as Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, or a vector database without changing the surrounding stage flow.
